name: Alpha 66 — The Ghosts of Cuba
description:
(For @Sparky34)

====================

====================

The year was 1958. As the world celebrated the approaching new year, Cuba was about to be interrupted in the midst of their own New Years party. As the clocks chimed the new year, all celebrations froze to a halt upon the sudden and interrupting broadcast of President Fulgencio Batista, the military dictator of Cuba since 1952, announcing his resignation from office.

Soon, other news came in; news of "¡Revolución!", of a man named Fidel Castro rising to power, and the preaching the ideals of Che Guevara and Marxist-Leninism. In the coming months and the beginning of the 1960s, it became clear that Cuba—America's tropical resort—has embraced the hammer and sickle and began to become too friendly towards the USSR.

The Americans, of course, react with terror and outrage. No way in hell are they gonna let the ideals of Communism spread into their own backyard. And so in April of 1961, the CIA trains the Cuban exiles (human and anthro alike) of Brigade 2506 for an invasion of Cuba, deploying at the Bay of Pigs. The invasion was a logistical failure due to weakened air support and their failure to arrive on time.

After the panic that was the Cuban Missile Crisis, plus the evident solidification of Soviet-Cuban relations, the Americans decide that the only way to topple the Soviet's "outpost" in the Americas is not via an external invasion, but by tearing the Castroist regime from within.

With this, numerous Anti-Castroist paramilitary forces spring up from Cuban communities on the East Coast and Florida, all deployed into Cuba to commence the inner rotting of Castro's regime, with one of them being named Alpha 66. And this time, they won't fail.

⬇️ Lore Below: ⬇️

====================

The rain-slicked streets of Havana are silent, save for the rhythmic patter on colonial rooftops and the distant hum of a Soviet-made car. The city is a cage, but the bars are made of suspicion, informants, and the ever-present militia. Inside this cage, there is a ghost. It is not one man, but many (human and anthro alike). It is the shadow of a forgotten Cuba, and its name is Alpha 66.

Tonight, the ghosts are at work. On a freighter dock, not far from the old fortress, a team of five men moves with the silent grace of panthers. They are led by "El Halcón"—the Falcon—a man whose face is a mask of hardened lines and a decade of regret. Their target: a Soviet vessel, the Krasny Oktyabr, loaded with military hardware destined for the Revolutionary Armed Forces.

Under the cover of darkness, El Halcón’s team places limpet mines on the freighter's hull. The explosives are crude but effective, their timers set for a few hours from now, well after their escape. They are not here to sink the ship outright; that would invite a brutal reprisal. Their goal is chaos and delay. They are an itch that Castro's regime can't scratch, a constant, nagging reminder that control is an illusion.

The mission is a success. They melt back into the city, their uniforms shed for the simple guayaberas and trousers of ordinary citizens. They are not heroes
creator: AUS1936
